text: After entering all required values for fields of the Dimensions AI database to ensure a focused retrieval of relevant academic articles49. In the AI database we searched for 5,103,170 articles (4,410,763 grants) in “Title” and “Abstract” fields with the following search terms: (“sex factor*” OR “sex characteristic*” OR “sex difference*” OR “gender factor*” OR “gender characteristic*” OR “gender difference*” OR “sex specific*” OR “gender specific*”) NOT (“sexual partner*” OR “sexual selection*” OR “sexual behavior*” OR “sexual behavior*”). From the 5,103,170 articles (4,410,763 grants) in the AI database we included 324,639 (21,250 grants) studies based on direct reflection of sex/gender characteristics in biomedical research in the fields of Biomedical and Clinical Sciences, Health Sciences, Psychology, Biological Sciences, Public Health, Biological Psychology, Health Service, Clinical and Health Psychology, and Clinical Science for which reflection of sex/gender trait is considered important1,42. Based on the inclusion criteria, the studies must have sex/gender-disaggregated data and or examine sex/gender differences in health outcomes and excluding studies related to sexual behavior and selection as indicated above. Data collection was performed for the period 2000 to 2021 as a basis for pre-policy and post-policy comparisons for the US, Canada, the UK and other countries with key gender responsive policies in place. The Dimensions AI platform was accessed on March 3, 2023 in order to get the final dataset. Statistics Research papers or grant reflecting sex/gender characteristics Key words Statistics (%) Measures the ratio of the total number of papers (and/or grant) published to the number of papers (or grant) containing the keyword as a percentage. For this analysis we use the several Statistical analysis techniques provided in Graph pad 10 in order to assess the impact of gender specific funding as a policy as well as the impact of gender specific journal editorial standards.
explanation: The passage has hallmarks of AI-generated drafts (repetitive phrasing, some awkward grammar, and placeholder-style citations) alongside plausible technical details, making it likely but not certain that it was AI-assisted.
